appDescription: Transform your mental wellness journey into a gamified adventure. Conquer OCD and anxiety with NeuroQuest.

dashboard.nextMissionDescription: Our AI has analyzed your recent activity to create a new challenge for you.

landing.headline: Transform Your Mind, Conquer Your Quest
landing.subheadline: NeuroQuest is a gamified mental wellness platform designed to help you manage intrusive thoughts, anxiety, and compulsions through proven therapeutic techniques.
landing.approachTitle: A New Approach to Mental Wellness
landing.approachDescription: Our platform integrates principles from CBT, ERP, and neuroscience into a structured, engaging, and supportive experience.
landing.feature1Description: Engage with exercises for thought logging, exposure therapy, and emotional regulation.
landing.feature2Description: Earn XP, level up, and unlock achievements as you build mental resilience.
landing.feature3Description: Receive personalized missions and coaching based on your unique patterns.
landing.feature4Description: Allow professionals to monitor progress and provide guided support.
landing.footerDisclaimer: This system does not replace clinical therapy.

personalizedMissionGenerator.genericError: Failed to generate mission. Please try again.
personalizedMissionGenerator.missionAcceptedDesc: Your new mission is available in Exposure Mode.

exposure.title: Exposure Mode (ERP)
exposure.description: Engage in progressively challenging Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) missions. This is a core part of overcoming OCD and anxiety.
exposure.progressDescription: You've completed {completed} out of {total} available missions.
exposure.lockedDescription: Complete more missions to unlock these challenges.
exposure.mission1Description: Touch an object you consider mildly contaminated (like a doorknob) and don't wash your hands for 5 minutes.
exposure.mission2Description: Leave your house for a short walk (5-10 minutes) without performing your usual checking rituals for the door or stove.
exposure.missionCompleted1Description: Acknowledge an urge to perform a minor compulsion and consciously decide not to do it.
exposure.missionLocked1Description: After using the restroom, wait 15 minutes before washing your hands.
exposure.noAvailableMissions: No available missions. Try generating a new one on the dashboard!
exposure.noCompletedMissions: You haven't completed any missions yet.

thoughtAnalyzer.title: Thought Analysis
thoughtAnalyzer.description: What's on your mind? Log it here to gain clarity.
thoughtAnalyzer.placeholder: e.g., 'I might have made a mistake at work that will get me fired.'
thoughtAnalyzer.validationError: Please enter a thought with at least 5 characters.
thoughtAnalyzer.genericError: Failed to analyze thought. Please try again.
thoughtAnalyzer.notAuthenticated: You must be logged in to analyze thoughts.
thoughtAnalyzer.thoughtLoggedDesc: Your thought has been successfully logged.

observer.title: Mental Observer
observer.description: Log your intrusive or distressing thoughts below. Our AI will help you analyze and reframe them from a CBT/ERP perspective.
observer.noThoughts: You haven't logged any thoughts yet. Use the tool above to get started.
observer.label: Label

progress.title: Your Progress
progress.description: Visualize your journey. Track your stats to see how far you've come.
progress.compulsionsChange: Resisted: +{value}% more compulsions resisted this month
progress.anxietyTrendDescription: Your average anxiety level over the last 7 days.
progress.compulsionTrackerDescription: Compulsions resisted vs. performed over time.

regulation.title: Emotional Regulation
regulation.description: Use these tools to manage acute anxiety and bring your nervous system back to a state of calm.
regulation.boxBreathingDescription: A simple technique to calm your nervous system.
regulation.fourSixBreathingDescription: Lengthen your exhale to activate the parasympathetic nervous system.

breathingExercise.startPrompt: Press start to begin the exercise.

reprogram.title: Cognitive Reprogramming
reprogram.description: Challenge catastrophic or magical thinking patterns. Enter a thought below, and our AI will guide you through a cognitive restructuring process to find a more balanced perspective.

cognitiveReprogrammer.title: Reprogramming Tool
cognitiveReprogrammer.description: Enter a thought you want to challenge.
cognitiveReprogrammer.placeholder: e.g., 'If I don't check the stove, the house will explode.'
cognitiveReprogrammer.validationError: Please enter a thought with at least 5 characters.
cognitiveReprogrammer.genericError: Failed to reprogram thought. Please try again.

crisis.title: Crisis Support
crisis.description: If you are in crisis or immediate danger, please use the resources below or call 911. Your safety is the top priority.
crisis.disclaimer: NeuroQuest is a tool for support and skill-building, not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

mainEmergency.title: Serious Emergency → 123
mainEmergency.desc: Main line and fastest general option (police, ambulances, firefighters, etc.)

mentalCrisis.title: Urgent Mental Crisis → 123 or psychiatric clinic
mentalCrisis.desc: For a severe mental crisis (risk of suicide, aggression, psychosis), call 123 immediately. You can also go to the emergency room of any hospital (they will refer you to psychiatry).

therapist.managePatients: Manage your patients and view their progress.
therapist.name: Name

login.title: Welcome to NeuroQuest
login.description: Sign in to begin your journey to mental wellness.
login.signInErrorDescription: Could not sign in with Google. Please check if pop-ups are blocked or try again later.
login.guestSignInErrorDescription: Could not sign in as a guest. Please try again later.

grounding.title: Grounding Exercises
grounding.description: A collection of grounding techniques to help you anchor yourself in the present moment during times of distress.

ex1.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique
ex1.desc: Name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste. This brings you into the present moment.

ex2.title: Mindful Breathing
ex2.desc: Focus on the sensation of your breath entering and leaving your body. Don't change it, just observe it for a minute or two.

ex3.title: Hold a Comfort Object
ex3.desc: Pick up an object and focus on its texture, weight, and temperature. A smooth stone, a soft fabric, or a stress ball works well.

ex4.title: Describe Your Environment
ex4.desc: Look around and describe your surroundings in detail, either silently to yourself or out loud. What colors and shapes do you see?

ex5.title: Listen to a Song
ex5.desc: Put on a piece of music and really listen to it. Try to identify different instruments, the rhythm, and the melody.

ex6.title: Stretch Your Body
ex6.desc: Gently stretch your arms, legs, and neck. Pay attention to the feeling of your muscles lengthening and releasing tension.

ex1.title: Temperature Change
ex1.desc: Hold a piece of ice in your hand or splash your face with cold water. The sudden temperature shift can jolt your system back to the present.

ex2.title: Strong Scent
ex2.desc: Inhale a strong, pleasant scent like peppermint oil, coffee grounds, or a citrus peel. The intense aroma can cut through overwhelming thoughts.

ex3.title: Body Scan
ex3.desc: Mentally scan your body from your toes to your head, noticing any sensations of tension, warmth, or coolness without judgment.

ex4.title: Push Against a Wall
ex4.desc: Stand and firmly push your hands against a wall for 15-30 seconds. Focus on the feeling of resistance and the strength in your muscles.

ex5.title: The Categories Game
ex5.desc: Pick a category (like 'fruits', 'countries', or 'animals') and name as many items in that category as you can. This engages your thinking brain.

ex6.title: Eat Something Mindfully
ex6.desc: Take a small piece of food, like a raisin or a nut, and eat it very slowly. Notice its texture, taste, and the sensation of chewing.

ex7.title: Grip and Release
ex7.desc: Clench your fists as tightly as you can for 5 seconds, then release the tension. Repeat this with different muscle groups (feet, shoulders).

ex1.title: Intense Temperature (TIPP Skill)
ex1.desc: Holding your breath, dip your face into a bowl of cold water for 15-30 seconds. This activates the mammalian dive reflex to rapidly calm your system.

ex2.title: Intense Exercise
ex2.desc: Engage in a short burst of intense physical activity, like doing 15 jumping jacks, running in place, or shadow boxing. This helps burn off excess adrenaline.

ex3.title: Sour or Spicy Taste
ex3.desc: Bite into a lemon wedge or eat something with a strong, spicy flavor. The intense sensation provides a powerful sensory anchor.

ex4.title: Grounding Through Feet
ex4.desc: Stand barefoot and focus all your attention on the soles of your feet. Feel the texture of the floor and imagine roots growing from your feet into the earth.

ex5.title: Recite Something Verbally
ex5.desc: Say a familiar poem, a phone number, a song's lyrics, or just the alphabet out loud. The act of speaking and recalling engages your brain differently.

ex6.title: Anchor Phrase
ex6.desc: Repeat a simple, factual phrase to yourself, such as 'My name is [Your Name]. I am in [Your City]. It is [Day of the week].' This reorients you to reality.

ex7.title: Paired Muscle Relaxation
ex7.desc: Tense a muscle group (like your hands) while you breathe in, and then relax it completely as you breathe out. Move through different muscle groups.

challenges.title: Cognitive Distortion Challenge
challenges.description: Test your ability to identify common cognitive distortions. Read the thought and select the distortion that best fits.

questions.thought: I got a 'B' on the test. I'm a complete failure.
questions.explanation: This is Black-and-White Thinking because it sees a situation in only two categories (total success or complete failure) instead of on a spectrum.

questions.thought: My boss didn't say hello this morning. They must be mad at me.
questions.explanation: This is Mind Reading, a form of Jumping to Conclusions, because you're assuming you know what someone else is thinking without sufficient evidence.

questions.thought: I felt anxious during the presentation, so I'm sure everyone thought I was incompetent.
questions.explanation: This is Emotional Reasoning, where you assume that because you feel a certain way, it must be true. 'I feel it, therefore it must be true.'

questions.thought: If I fail this one project, my entire career will be ruined.
questions.explanation: This is Catastrophizing (or Magnification), where you expect the worst-case scenario to happen, blowing things out of proportion.

questions.thought: I made one mistake. Now I'll never get it right.
questions.explanation: This is Overgeneralization. You're taking a single negative event and turning it into a never-ending pattern of defeat.

medication.title: Medical Decision Support
medication.description: Clinical guidelines for pharmacological treatment based on diagnosis and severity. For professional use only.
medication.warningTitle: Professional Use Only - Not a Substitute for Medical Evaluation
medication.warningDescription: This system does not replace a professional medical evaluation. Doses must be defined and prescribed by a qualified physician. Do not self-medicate.

rules.1: Do not define doses automatically; use referential ranges according to clinical guidelines and validated by a physician.
rules.2: Requires professional medical validation to start, adjust, or suspend any treatment.
rules.3: Alert for suicide risk when starting antidepressants, especially in young people.
rules.4: Prioritize emergency care in case of crisis, risk of self-harm or harm to others.

checkIn.title: Mental Check-In
checkIn.subtitle: Evaluate your emotional state and receive a personalized mission to improve your mental well-being.
checkIn.subtitle_marketing: Discover how your mind is today and what to do to improve it step by step.
checkIn.importantText: This tool does not replace a medical or psychological diagnosis. It is a guide for self-assessment and orientation.
checkIn.alertText: Your response suggests significant discomfort or possible emotional risk. Seek professional support as soon as possible and contact a trusted person right now.
checkIn.disclaimerText: This tool provides general guidance based on self-reported symptoms. It does not substitute for a professional medical, psychiatric, or psychological diagnosis.
checkIn.validationError: Please answer all questions before submitting.

questions.text: Do you feel nervous, anxious, or on edge for no clear reason?
Have you lost interest or pleasure in activities you used to enjoy?
Do you have trouble sleeping or do you sleep too much?
Do you feel tired or lacking energy most of the time?
Do you find it hard to concentrate or make simple decisions?
Do you feel sad, empty, or hopeless?
Are you easily irritated or do you overreact?
Do you avoid social situations or important activities?
Do you have recurring negative thoughts about yourself?
Have you had thoughts of harming yourself or giving up?

moderate.description: CBT, gradual exposure, and emotional regulation.
tasks: Gradual exposure to an avoided situation

severe.description: Close monitoring, professional support, and specialized evaluation.
tasks: Talk to a trusted person today
Create a basic emotional safety plan
